entry.title: Matchmaking
entry.summary:
Author: call911dumbass (heehooninc)

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,215Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 7

Summary: Christopher notices tension between his dad and Buck and decides to intervene, subtly setting up situations that force them to spend time together (movie nights, outings, etc.). What starts as lighthearted turns more serious when Christopher directly asks Eddie why he looks sad when Buck leaves. The simplicity of the question forces Eddie to confront feelings he’s been overcomplicating.

entry.title: trophy wife
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,105Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 0

Summary: “This is what you wanted, isn’t it?” Eddie’s voice was a low rumble, right behind him now. “Prancing in here looking like a dirty fantasy laid out on my goddamn office sofa, and you expect me to think about profit margins?” Buck finally turned his head, looking over his shoulder. Eddie’s face was a mask of strained control, his eyes dark with a hunger that made Buck’s stomach flip. “Is it working?” Buck asked, his voice breathy. Eddie’s answer was to let his hand slide higher, up the sensitive skin of Buck’s thigh, until his fingertips grazed the edge of the satin panties. /// Buck knew exactly how to get his workaholic husband to take a break.

entry.title: 𝑴𝒊𝒏𝒆 - 𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑙𝑖𝑐𝑖𝑡𝑙𝑦, 𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑣𝑖𝑡𝑎𝑏𝑙𝑦, 𝑒𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑛𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦
entry.summary:
Author: Graceful_Ashes

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 65,549Chapters: 23/?Kudos: 517

Summary: 𝑇ℎ𝑒𝑖𝑟 𝑙𝑖𝑣𝑒𝑠 𝑎𝑟𝑒 𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑡𝑤𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑑, 𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑥𝑡𝑟𝑖𝑐𝑎𝑏𝑙𝑦 𝑠𝑜. 𝐼𝑡'𝑠 𝑎 𝑡𝑟𝑢𝑡ℎ 𝐸𝑑𝑑𝑖𝑒 𝑘𝑛𝑜𝑤𝑠 𝑓𝑢𝑛𝑑𝑎𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦. 𝐼𝑡'𝑠 𝑎 𝑔𝑟𝑜𝑢𝑛𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑓𝑎𝑐𝑡 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘’𝑠 𝑟𝑒𝑙𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠ℎ𝑖𝑝. 𝑇ℎ𝑒𝑦'𝑟𝑒 𝑏𝑒𝑠𝑡 𝑓𝑟𝑖𝑒𝑛𝑑𝑠 𝑜𝑛 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑝𝑟𝑒𝑐𝑖𝑝𝑖𝑐𝑒 𝑜𝑓 𝑠𝑜𝑚𝑒𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑚𝑜𝑟𝑒 𝑎𝑛𝑑 ℎ𝑒'𝑠 𝑖𝑛 𝑛𝑜 𝑟𝑢𝑠ℎ 𝑡𝑜 𝑝𝑢𝑠ℎ 𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑚 𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑜 𝑢𝑛𝑐ℎ𝑎𝑟𝑡𝑒𝑑 𝑤𝑎𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑠. 𝑇ℎ𝑒𝑦'𝑙𝑙 𝑓𝑖𝑛𝑑 𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑖𝑟 𝑤𝑎𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑟𝑒 𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑢𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦, 𝑖𝑡'𝑠 𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑣𝑖𝑡𝑎𝑏𝑙𝑒. 𝑆𝑜 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘 𝑐𝑎𝑛 𝑑𝑎𝑡𝑒 𝑜𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑟 𝑝𝑒𝑜𝑝𝑙𝑒 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑛𝑜𝑤, ℎ𝑒 𝑐𝑎𝑛 ℎ𝑎𝑣𝑒 ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑓𝑢𝑛. 𝐸𝑑𝑑𝑖𝑒 𝑤𝑜𝑛'𝑡 𝑠𝑝𝑜𝑖𝑙 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑓𝑜𝑟 ℎ𝑖𝑚. 𝐼𝑛 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑒𝑛𝑑, 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘 𝑎𝑙𝑤𝑎𝑦𝑠 𝑐𝑜𝑚𝑒𝑠 ℎ𝑜𝑚𝑒 𝑡𝑜 ℎ𝑖𝑚. 𝑂𝑟 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐸𝑑𝑑𝑖𝑒 𝑎𝑟𝑒 𝑙𝑖𝑣𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡𝑜𝑔𝑒𝑡ℎ𝑒𝑟 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘 ℎ𝑎𝑠 𝑠𝑡𝑎𝑟𝑡𝑒𝑑 𝑑𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑎𝑔𝑎𝑖𝑛. 𝐸𝑑𝑑𝑖𝑒 𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑠𝑒𝑠 ℎ𝑒'𝑠 𝑖𝑛 𝑙𝑜𝑣𝑒 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝐵𝑢𝑐𝑘 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑡𝑎𝑘𝑒𝑠 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑒 𝑡𝑜 𝑤𝑜𝑟𝑘 𝑡ℎ𝑟𝑜𝑢𝑔ℎ ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑓𝑒𝑒𝑙𝑖𝑛𝑔𝑠 - 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑎𝑡 𝑎𝑙𝑙 𝑡ℎ𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑡𝑒𝑛𝑒𝑑 𝑏𝑦 ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑏𝑒𝑠𝑡 𝑓𝑟𝑖𝑒𝑛𝑑'𝑠 𝑙𝑜𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑠.

entry.title: Holding the Line-You Don’t Get to Leave
entry.summary:
Author: Accaliux

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,628Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 48

Summary: He reaches for Buck, their fingers intertwine And that’s when the whole car lurches. A violent yank hits his harness as Ravi and Chim’s line system reacts. Buck is ripped backward toward the surface, his hand losing Eddie’s. “No!” ... “There’s no tension!” Ravi yells. “Cap, there’s nothing on Eddie’s line!”   or After a rescue call leaves Eddie critically injured, Buck finds himself holding together everything Eddie holds dear Waiting for Eddie to wake up might be the hardest rescue Buck has ever faced.

entry.title: one single glimpse of relief
entry.summary:
Author: greyspilot

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 89,974Chapters: 16/20Kudos: 211

Summary: "What if I never get better? What if I always feel like this?" Buck finally looks back up at him, vulnerability and compassion clear on his too-expressive face. "I don't know," he says honestly. "I don't…" he shudders, gives a tiny shrug of his shoulders and tries again. "I don't have an answer for that, man. It's been seven months for me and I still feel…like this." "Like what?" Buck just shrugs again, then mumbles, "Not myself." And Eddie doesn't know what to say to that either, because this is the only version of Buck he's ever known.Or: Buck and Eddie meet at grief group.

entry.title: Last chances and second glances
entry.summary:
Author: thebookofcrows

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 175,085Chapters: 52/60Kudos: 891

Summary: St. Anthony’s was his last chance, and Evan knew that. He had been kicked out of five schools in the past six years, and after his sophomore year ended with a meeting between his parents and his last headmaster that could be summarized with: “we’re not kicking him out, but just know that Evan is not welcome back in September.” His parents wanted to send him to military school.       Evan knew he couldn’t survive military school, that would be his last drop, the one that finally shattered the vase. He couldn’t be the type of person that would be able to put himself back together once they broke him down.     This had to work. . . . Buck is the new kid in an El Paso boarding school, trying his best to stay out of trouble. Eddie is a rockstar (in the world of high school popularity) he's captain to the football team, he's dating the most popular girl in school, and looking at a full ride sports scholarship in the fall. He should feel like he won at life, which is why he doesn't understand why the only time he feels alive is when he catches a glimpse of the new guy on the running team. Or in simpler terms: Buddie high-school AU!

entry.title: Open Your Eyes
entry.summary:
Author: xDnicki

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,031Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 128

Summary: "There was this moment. Bonnie said that you'd stop looking for me at some point. That'd you'd give up -on me," Buck's voice now thick with the tears he is trying to hold back. "And I thought maybe you would. After my behaviour in the diner." (On the 10 hour ride home Buck has some feelings. Also Christopher says "What the fuck, Dad." When they come home because "Dad, this does not look like a minor accident.")

entry.title: objects in the mirror are larger than they appear
entry.summary:
Author: madforyou

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,758Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 258

Summary: Oh, alright, cool. Cool, cool, cool. Neat. That is a photograph of Eddie Diaz's dick. It's just there. On the screen. In his hand. In high resolution. His best friend’s dick is in his hand. Well, wait — the photo. The photo of the penis is in his hand. Eddie’s dick isn’t in his hand. That would be absurd. It’s just a penis. Buck has one of those. So does Eddie, apparently and it’s… Buck has seen it, absorbed it, and every thought he's ever had has been replaced by a high-pitched tone and the image in front of him, which is — and this is the last coherent observation he'll produce for the foreseeable future — Big. Genuinely, impressively big. Buck closes out of the photo and locks the phone so hard he's surprised it doesn't crack in half. "Maddie say anything?" Eddie asks. "Yep," Buck squeaks. Like a mouse. A giant, shocked mouse that has just seen its best friend’s erect dick. "You okay?" "Great. Y-yep. So great. I-I'm just really— the sunset was really— it's very— yep." "It’s yep?" "Uh-huh. Very yep." Or,Stumbling upon your straight best friends' nudes is fine. Buck is fine. Everything is fine.

entry.title: Secret succession
entry.summary:
Author: OLKOLO

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,816Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 38

Summary: Lady Caroline Collingwood adopts a neglected American toddler named Evan Buckley during a trip to the United States and brings him back to England, renaming him Oliver Roy and raising him within the powerful Roy family from Succession. Growing up surrounded by wealth and ruthless family politics under Logan Roy, Oliver becomes intelligent, observant, and strategically brilliant. At 26, after conflicts and power struggles within the Roy family, Oliver leaves and moves to Los Angeles. There he hides his identity by returning to his birth name, Evan Buckley, and builds a new life as a firefighter in the world of 9-1-1. He lives quietly until his true identity as a member of the Roy dynasty is eventually discovered, bringing his two very different worlds—powerful media empire and everyday heroism—into collision.

entry.title: The Buck Effect
entry.summary:
Author: Fictionwriter

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 20,941Chapters: 6/7Kudos: 4

Summary: Science has said that one singular event can ultimately result in later much larger consequences. Eddie Diaz discovers the truth of this when he meets Evan (Buck) Buckley, and the trajectory of his life changes forever. It’s 1920 and the Great War has ended. Eddie Diaz, with his son Christopher, make the decision to move from Texas to the growing metropolis of Los Angeles. Former Army medic Eddie has a new job, a new career and, he hopes, a new direction.

entry.title: safe and sound
entry.summary:
Author: IllustrationofWinter

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,512Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 80

Summary: They had both gotten cleaned up at the hospital, but they wanted out of their itchy hospital-issued clothes. At least some of their own clothes came out unscathed in all of this. Eddie wasn’t sure why he found that so funny. “I’m glad something came out okay.” He hadn’t meant to say it out loud. Or maybe he did. Maybe he needed to hear Buck’s voice. Needed to hear a stupid joke to know that he was actually okay. It got a huff of a laugh. “Yeah, I guess. But of course it wasn’t my favorite shirt that made it.” It was a joke. Eddie knew Buck’s favorite shirt was back at his house, safe and sound. Or, he should say shirts. Buck really seemed to like Eddie’s shirts, even if they did run a little tight on him. It always did funny things to Eddie’s heart to see Buck had stolen another shirt. To see Buck actually wearing them. Eddie sighed, dropping his head before giving Buck a small smile. “I was trying to add to the pros here, man.” “Call me a glass-half-empty kind of guy, I guess." Buck shrugged. OR: A 9x13 coda

entry.title: One Grey Hoodie and a Disco Cowboy Hat
entry.summary:
Author: shrimprangoon

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 11,502Chapters: 3/4Kudos: 174

Summary: He feels unhinged. He feels like all his bones are unspooling into piles of spaghetti that someone then tazed. Oh god- "Does Eddie know he's gay?!!" "You don't even know he's gay," Hen says calmly. "Not for sure. You can't assume these things unless you talk to him, Buck." "That's why he doesn't want to date!" Buck plows on, Hen's words already buried under ten million gay possibilities. "Why he hasn't looked at a woman in over a year. Why he was always so bad at dating despite being the hottest person to walk this earth." "Okay, I think we're getting a bit carried away here." "Hen, he keeps wearing my clothes." “He- what?" OR Buck is determined to give Eddie a fun, relaxing trip to Nashville for the firefighter games and can be so totally normal about his best friend wearing his clothes and letting his guard down and also maybe possible being gay.
